message:
refactor(config): Improve repo detection and simplify config merging

This commit modernizes the configuration loading system with better
repository context awareness and cleaner merge semantics.

## Repository Context Detection

Replaced the simple file-existence check with git-based detection:

- New detectRepoContext() function uses `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`
  to reliably identify the repository root
- Only considers .cass directories within the actual git repository
- Returns structured context: { inRepo: boolean, repoRoot?: string, cassDir?: string }

This ensures that .cass configs are only loaded when actually inside a git
repository, preventing false positives from leftover directories.

## Configuration Loading Improvements

- Removed the separate deepMergeConfig() helper function
- Implemented inline merging with explicit deep merge for nested objects:
  - sanitization: Preserves all levels of override precedence
  - scoring: New deep merge ensures per-project scoring customization works
- Renamed normalizeKeys() to normalizeConfigKeys() for clarity
- Simplified saveConfig() to write directly without temp file dance

## Default Value Updates

- Changed default model from claude-sonnet-4-20250514 to claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022
  (the former was a typo/non-existent model ID)
- Updated minFeedbackForActive from 1 to 3, requiring more evidence before
  a bullet transitions from candidate to established status
- Removed auditLevel from sanitization config (unused complexity)

## Import Changes

- Added exec from child_process and promisify from util for async shell commands
- Added log to imports from utils.js for debug output

## Structural Reorganization

- Moved getSanitizeConfig() and ensureConfigDirs() earlier in file
- Grouped related functions together (defaults, paths, repo detection, loading)
- Cleaner function signatures and reduced nesting
